meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no understanding of APIs (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing & API Integration: A Student's Guide
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between code and revenue. Learn to integrate Stripe APIs for SaaS pricing using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert-led tutorial.
content.answer_block: To master SaaS pricing without prior API knowledge, you must decouple your billing logic from your core application. By leveraging Stripe's SDKs via Antigravity and automating implementation with Claude Code, you transform complex API calls into simple, manageable functions, ensuring a production-ready, scalable revenue model for your portfolio apps.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks perfecting your UI, sweating over every pixel, only to hit a wall that separates hobbyist scripts from legitimate SaaS products: the API. The daunting void of request headers, webhooks, and authentication tokens feels like a barrier to entry, leaving your projects feeling incomplete and unmonetized.

But here is the truth: APIs are just messengers. Once you stop fearing the documentation and start automating the handshake, you unlock the ability to charge for your value. Today, we bridge that gap. We are using Antigravity and Claude Code to weave Stripe’s robust pricing infrastructure directly into your app, turning your code into a business.

steps.title: Define Your Pricing Schema
steps.description: Before touching code, map your product tiers to Stripe API objects. We define the business logic first so the implementation becomes a mechanical process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a 3-tier SaaS pricing model (Free, Pro, Enterprise) compatible with Stripe's Product and Price objects. Include fields for recurring intervals and metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a stripe_schema.json based on the antigravity pricing definition'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Billing Service
steps.description: Create an isolated service layer to handle Stripe communications, preventing your main codebase from becoming a tangled web of API calls.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular billing service class in TypeScript that initializes the Stripe SDK and provides a secure method to create a Checkout Session.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add ./src/services/billing.ts --context 'use stripe-node and ensure error handling for failed API requests'

steps.title: Secure Webhook Implementation
steps.description: Webhooks are how Stripe talks back to your app. We implement a secure listener to handle subscription status changes automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Express.js webhook handler that verifies Stripe signatures and updates the user subscription status in our database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./src/controllers/webhook.ts --prompt 'add signature verification logic using stripe.webhooks.constructEvent'

steps.title: Frontend Checkout Integration
steps.description: Connect your UI to the billing service. This step turns your 'Upgrade' button into a functional revenue driver.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component that calls our billing service and redirects the user to the Stripe Checkout portal based on the selected tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create ./src/components/PricingCard.tsx --context 'integrate with the billing service created in step 2'

steps.title: Testing the Payment Flow
steps.description: Simulate end-to-end transactions using Stripe's test mode to ensure your logic survives real-world edge cases.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test suite for the billing service that mocks Stripe API responses for success, payment failure, and subscription cancellation.

pro_tips: Always use Stripe's 'Price IDs' from the dashboard, never hardcode prices in your frontend; it prevents sync issues.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your database schema to handle short-term payment failures without immediately locking out users.
Use environment variables for all API keys, and never, ever commit a .env file to your repository.
Log every webhook event to a separate database table for 30 days to debug 'missing subscription' issues during development.
Focus on 'Idempotency Keys' for API requests to ensure that double-clicks on your checkout button don't create multiple invoices.
content.next_steps: Now that you have a functional billing system, deploy your app to Vercel or Railway, verify your webhooks in live mode, and launch your SaaS to the first 10 users.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a coder; you are a product builder with the power to monetize your craft. This skill set is the missing link in your portfolio. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
